title: Sprint
description: Source-of-truth file for an AGF sprint. `BACKLOG.md` and the rendered section of `BACKLOG_ARCHIVE.md` are derived views over the set of `backlog/sprints/S<NN>.sprint.json` files. Editing the rendered Markdown directly is a `backlog:check` failure.

agfFormatVersion.description: AGF schema version. Bumped only when the sprint file shape changes incompatibly.

id.description: Sprint id, e.g. `S078`. Zero-padded to keep the filename + lexical sort happy.

title.description: Short human-readable sprint title (≤80 chars recommended).

status.description: Sprint lifecycle. `pending` = planned but not started; `active` = current sprint (at most one across all files); `archived` = closed.

startedAt.description: ISO date when the sprint moved to `active`.

archivedAt.description: ISO date when the sprint moved to `archived`.

stories.description: Stories planned, in-progress, or completed inside this sprint.

followUps.description: Items discovered during the sprint that didn't fit; promoted into the next sprint's candidates at archive time.

outOfScope.description: Items the sprint explicitly chose not to address; for archive readers who want to understand the boundary.

prUrl.description: Pull request that closed the sprint.

notes.description: Free-text observations the sprint wants to preserve (e.g. measurement summaries, links to research notes).

id.description: Story id, all-caps kebab. Stable across sprints; commit messages should start with `<id>:`.

status.description: Story lifecycle. `implemented` requires `verification` to be non-empty; `deferred` requires a `deferredReason`.

summary.description: One-paragraph summary of what shipped (for implemented stories) or what's planned.

deliverables.description: Key files / surfaces touched by the story. Can be derived from `git log` once `BACKLOG-git-derived` lands.

verification.description: Commands or behaviours that prove the story works. Required when `status: "implemented"`.

dependsOn.description: Story ids (within this sprint OR cross-sprint) that must reach `implemented` before this one can start.

epic.description: Optional epic id this story rolls up to. Used by `HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md` cross-references.
